scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within bioinformatics data processing scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: Visium and Xenium spatial mapping approaches are methodologically sound; scale factor handling and coordinate projection are correctly implemented.

code_usability.detail: Script compiles cleanly; optional imports with fallbacks prevent hard failures on missing dependencies. Platform auto-detection documented in Workflow Step 2 but no detect_platform function found in script — file-based detection logic is present in class methods but not exposed as a standalone auto-detect path.

functional_suitability.note: Non-10x platform escape hatch in Input Validation; platform auto-detection in Workflow Step 2.

reliability.note: Platform auto-detection closes the missing --platform gap; Fallback Template includes platform identification guidance.

performance_context.note: Token usage is proportional to input complexity; execution overhead is acceptable for bioinformatics data processing tasks though room for compression exists.

agent_usability.note: Platform auto-detection in Workflow Step 2; non-10x platform redirect in Input Validation.

human_usability.note: When-to-Use and When-Not-to-Use sections are clearly stated; error scenarios and recovery paths are documented for typical bioinformatics data processing use cases.

security.note: No credential exposure or injection vectors identified; input handling stays within bioinformatics data processing boundaries.

maintainability.note: The skill is modular and self-contained, with low overhead for future updates to bioinformatics data processing logic or output formats.

agent_specific.note: Platform auto-detection and non-10x escape hatch close the two key v2 gaps; composability note documents downstream integration points.

key_strengths: Platform auto-detection in Workflow Step 2 — closes the v2 missing --platform gap
Non-10x platform escape hatch (Slide-seq, MERFISH, seqFISH) in Input Validation
Dual-platform support (Visium and Xenium) with correct platform-specific data loading
Stress-Case Output Checklist ensures complex multi-constraint requests are fully addressed
HTML report generation provides publication-ready output

recommendations.title: Platform auto-detection not exposed as standalone CLI path in script
recommendations.problem: Workflow Step 2 documents platform auto-detection, but scripts/main.py has no detect_platform function or --platform auto mode. File-based detection logic exists inside class methods but is not reachable when --platform is omitted from the CLI.
recommendations.root_cause: Documentation fix applied to SKILL.md but the script was not updated to expose auto-detection as a CLI-accessible code path.
recommendations.fix: Add a detect_platform(data_dir) function in main.py that checks for filtered_feature_bc_matrix.h5+spatial/ (Visium) or transcripts.parquet (Xenium). Call it when --platform is not provided and report the inferred platform before proceeding.

recommendations.title: No explicit memory limit guidance for Xenium datasets
recommendations.problem: Large Xenium datasets can exceed available memory, but no specific memory threshold or subsampling guidance is documented.
recommendations.root_cause: Limitations section mentions --crop but does not quantify memory requirements for Xenium.
recommendations.fix: Add to Notes: 'Large Xenium datasets (>1M transcripts) may require 32GB+ RAM. Use --crop to limit the region of interest, or subsample transcripts before running.'
